dish_name: Stir-Fried Soba Noodles with Tofu and Green Vegetables
description: Stir-fried soba noodles with tofu, broccoli, green peas, mushrooms, and snow peas (or snap peas) come together in an easy vegan recipe. A quick, light dinner, it’s easy to make and pleasing to eat.
ingredients: [{"name": "soba noodles", "unit": "ounces", "amount": "8"}, {"name": "extra-firm tofu", "unit": "ounce tub", "amount": "14"}, {"name": "safflower or other high-heat vegetable oil", "unit": "tablespoons", "amount": "2"}, {"name": "soy sauce or tamari", "unit": "tablespoon", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "garlic, minced", "unit": "cloves", "amount": "2 to 3"}, {"name": "small broccoli florets", "unit": "cups", "amount": "3"}, {"name": "snow peas or snap peas, trimmed", "unit": "cup or so", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "brown or white mushrooms, cleaned, stemmed, and sliced", "unit": "cup or so", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "frozen (thawed) green peas", "unit": "cup", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "grated fresh or squeeze-bottle ginger", "unit": "teaspoons", "amount": "2"}, {"name": "teriyaki marinade", "unit": "tablespoons", "amount": "3 to 4"}, {"name": "Freshly ground pepper", "unit": null, "amount": "to taste"}, {"name": "scallions, thinly sliced", "unit": "optional", "amount": "2 to 3"}, {"name": "sesame seeds", "unit": "tablespoon optional", "amount": "1"}]
instructions: Cook the noodles according to package directions until al dente, then drain. Cut the tofu into 6 slabs crosswise, blot very well on paper towels or clean tea towels, then cut each slab into 1/2-inch dice. Heat half of the oil and soy sauce together in a stir-fry pan. Add the tofu and stir-fry over medium-high heat until golden on most sides, stirring often, about 8 to 10 minutes. Remove to a plate. Let the pan cool a bit before heating the remaining oil. In the same pan, sauté the garlic for a minute or so. Add the broccoli, snow peas, mushrooms, and peas. Turn the heat back up and stir-fry for 2 to 3 minutes, or until the broccoli and snow peas are bright green and tender-crisp. If the pan dries out, add a small amount of water. Stir in the ginger, then return the tofu to the pan along with the cooked noodles. Add teriyaki marinade and pepper to taste and stir-fry just until everything is melded together. Stir in sesame seeds and/or scallions, if using, and serve at once.
notes: Feel free to substitute udon or even regular spaghetti. Variations: use whatever vegetables you have on hand such as green beans, corn, cabbage, leafy greens, and more.
tags: vegan, stir-fry, soba noodles, tofu, green vegetables, quick dinner
